meta.purpose: Public, verifiable government/institutional research and data that ground FarmGraph Rakshak design decisions. Every entry states what is used, where, and under what access type. Nothing here implies live API connectivity.

evidence.claim: Jodhpur (IMD station 42339) climate normals: annual mean total rainfall 370.2 mm; July 119.7 mm and August 113.5 mm (≈63% of annual rain in two months); ~20.9 rainy days/year; mean annual daily-max 34.0°C.
evidence.institution: India Meteorological Department (IMD), Ministry of Earth Sciences
evidence.source: IMD Jaipur — station climatological extremes/normals, Jodhpur 42339 (PDF)
evidence.usedIn: Rationale for the outbreak weather-suitability component: downy mildew risk concentrates in the Jul–Aug humid window; pilot calendar aligns to kharif sowing after monsoon onset.
evidence.honestyNote: Normals are context only. The live weather-suitability component is a seeded placeholder until a district-level IMD feed is wired (adapter PUBLIC_DATA_ONLY).

evidence.claim: Jodhpur district: IMD normal rainfall 314 mm; 44-year (1970–2014) average 373.7 mm; July+August deliver ~68% of rainfall; the district suffered moderate droughts in 1974, 1984–87, 1991, 1993, 2009 and severe drought in 2002.
evidence.institution: Central Ground Water Board (CGWB), Government of India
evidence.source: Jodhpur District groundwater information booklet (government publication citing IMD)
evidence.usedIn: docs/problem-research.md — why rainfed pest/disease surveillance in these districts must assume drought-stressed, low-biomass crops and compressed scouting windows.
evidence.honestyNote: District-level context, not a real-time data feed.

evidence.claim: AICPMIP-recommended pearl millet downy mildew management integrates: resistant cultivars; hybrid/variety rotation; seed treatment research (e.g. Apron 35 SD); roguing of infected plants with burying/burning; foliar intervention thresholds researched at 2–5% infection.
evidence.institution: ICAR — All India Coordinated Pearl Millet Improvement Project (AICPMIP)
evidence.source: AICPMIP — Pearl Millet Diseases (official project site)
evidence.usedIn: Golden advisory ADV-2601 'Do now' steps (rogue out and bury/burn infected plants; improve airflow) mirror the published cultural-control recommendations. Expert escalation triggers follow the researched low-threshold logic.
evidence.honestyNote: Chemical names/doses exist in the cited research but are deliberately NOT surfaced in the app — chemical content stays locked behind approved, CIB&RC-aligned advisories.

evidence.claim: ICRISAT's pearl millet downy mildew management monograph documents IDM modules combining moderate host resistance, reduced-dose metalaxyl seed treatment, and bioagents (Bacillus pumilus INR 7, Trichoderma harzianum, Pseudomonas fluorescens, chitosan), validated across AICPMIP locations.
evidence.source: Downy Mildew of Pearl Millet and its Management (ICRISAT Open Access Repository)
evidence.usedIn: Taxonomy design: downy_mildew is modelled as a high-spread-risk condition that always escalates to expert review; monitoring steps (re-inspect every 48h, re-photograph marked plants) reflect the disease's humidity-driven sporulation cycle documented here.
evidence.honestyNote: Research lineage is cited for credibility; no treatment dose is encoded in the product.

evidence.claim: Three-year kharif field trials (2021–2023, JAU Jamnagar) found Trichoderma harzianum seed treatment (8 g/kg) statistically at par with metalaxyl (6 g/kg) for downy mildew incidence (~10–12% vs 18.6% control).
evidence.institution: Junagadh Agricultural University — Pearl Millet Research Station
evidence.source: Chaudhari et al. 2024, Management of Pearl Millet Downy Mildew Disease by Organic Practices
evidence.usedIn: Advisory safety posture: credible non-chemical options exist, which is why immediate farmer-facing steps can be genuinely useful while chemical content remains locked.
evidence.honestyNote: Single-station trial evidence; advisory versions require KVK expert approval before issue.

evidence.claim: data.gov.in (Open Government Data Platform India, NIC) exposes 100,000+ datasets as REST resources via api.data.gov.in/resource/{index} with a free registered API key (registration on data.gov.in 'My Account'; sample/demo key format published).
evidence.institution: National Informatics Centre (NIC), MeitY — OGD Platform India
evidence.source: data.gov.in developer documentation and community wrappers
evidence.usedIn: AGMARKNET/e-NAM adapter: market-context data is reachable TODAY through the public OGD API with a free key — no MoU required. This is the strongest real integration available to a Task-001-stage product.
evidence.honestyNote: Adapter remains PUBLIC_DATA_ONLY in this repo: no key is committed and no live calls are made. The integration matrix documents the concrete path (register key → resource index → district filter).

evidence.claim: Bhashini (Digital India Bhashini Division, MeitY) exposes language AI through the ULCA platform: pipeline APIs supporting ASR / NMT / TTS task sequences (ASR+NMT+TTS), model IDs from IIT/IITM/IIITH/CDAC research groups, onboarding via the Udyat portal; published API docs describe pipeline search → config → compute call flow.
evidence.institution: Digital India Bhashini Division (MeitY), built on AI4Bharat/ULCA research
evidence.usedIn: Bhashini adapter contract: voice pipeline shape (ASR+NMT+TTS) is designed against the published ULCA pipeline flow.
evidence.honestyNote: Marwari/Mewari are dialects without scheduled-language ASR coverage; independent 2026 assessments place dialect voice at research stage. The prototype ships Hindi/English with typed regional vocabulary and never claims dialect ASR.

evidence.claim: Natural Earth 1:10m admin-1 boundaries are public domain and include the Rajasthan state outline used for the pilot geospatial view.
evidence.institution: Natural Earth (community project, public domain terms)
evidence.usedIn: data/geo/rajasthan-outline.geojson (simplified) — the only boundary the map draws.
evidence.honestyNote: District polygons are deliberately not shown: a licence-clean, survey-quality district source was not established; district HQs render as labelled reference points instead.
